# T02: Made the fix-first admin routers self-guarding and centralized structured-log redaction for token/password/cookie/email.

**Made the fix-first admin routers self-guarding and centralized structured-log redaction for token/password/cookie/email.**

## What Happened

I closed the first concrete admin-security seams from the S03 inventory instead of leaving them dependent on wiring outside the modules. In `backend/src/admin/api/admin.py`, `analytics.py`, `release_verification.py`, `system_logs.py`, and `training_records.py`, I replaced the legacy `Depends(get_current_user)` entries with `Depends(get_current_admin_user)` so those routers enforce admin-only access even when mounted without `main.py`’s wrapper dependencies. I then added focused isolated-router regression proof in `backend/tests/integration/test_admin_users_api.py` that mounts each router on a throwaway FastAPI app without global guards and verifies non-admin callers still receive the structured `[ROLE_REQUIRED]` deny payload; while touching that file, I also updated stale auth-contract assertions that still expected string details instead of the current structured `detail={error,message}` shape. On the log-safety side, I turned `backend/src/common/monitoring/logger.py` into the shared redaction boundary by recursively sanitizing token/password/cookie/email fields, including nested `extra` metadata, masking email local-parts while fully redacting the other secret classes. `backend/src/common/auth/api.py` and `backend/src/common/auth/service.py` now emit structured auth/logout/reset/token-verification fields instead of stringifying credential-path exceptions or raw emails, so auth observability remains usable without leaking sensitive values. Finally, I refreshed `backend/src/admin/api/security_inventory.py` and `backend/src/common/monitoring/log_safety_inventory.py` so the code-owned baseline reflects the new reality: the former fix-first admin route families and sensitive-log sinks are now marked as closed, and the fix-first import check returns zero remaining surfaces.

## Verification

Fresh proof covered both the new behavior and the slice gate. I first ran the new isolated-router RBAC regression subset in `backend/tests/integration/test_admin_users_api.py`; it finished 5/5 green and proved the five fix-first admin router modules deny non-admin callers even when `main.py` does not wrap them. I then ran the new shared logger redaction subset in `backend/tests/unit/admin/test_admin_users_api_models.py`; it finished 2/2 green and proved the shared logger redacts top-level and nested token/password/cookie/email fields while preserving safe metadata. After fixing one stale structured-detail assertion uncovered by the exact gate, I ran the task-plan command `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/integration/test_admin_users_api.py backend/tests/unit/admin/test_admin_users_api_models.py -x -q`; it finished 33/33 green. I also re-imported `FIX_FIRST_ADMIN_ROUTE_FAMILIES` and `FIX_FIRST_SENSITIVE_LOG_SURFACES` from the backend runtime and confirmed both counts are now `0`, which directly verifies the code-owned security inventory baseline is closed. Fresh LSP diagnostics reported no issues on the touched admin/auth/logger/inventory/test files.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/integration/test_admin_users_api.py -k 'admin_router_modules_require_admin_even_without_main_router_guard' -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3362ms |
| 2 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/admin/test_admin_users_api_models.py -k 'sanitize_log_kwargs' -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 1781ms |
| 3 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/integration/test_admin_users_api.py backend/tests/unit/admin/test_admin_users_api_models.py -x -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 4106ms |
| 4 | `backend/venv/bin/python - <<'INNER'
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, 'backend/src')
from admin.api.security_inventory import FIX_FIRST_ADMIN_ROUTE_FAMILIES
from common.monitoring.log_safety_inventory import FIX_FIRST_SENSITIVE_LOG_SURFACES
print({'fix_first_admin_route_families': len(FIX_FIRST_ADMIN_ROUTE_FAMILIES), 'fix_first_sensitive_log_surfaces': len(FIX_FIRST_SENSITIVE_LOG_SURFACES)})
INNER` | 0 | ✅ pass | 959ms |

## Deviations

Local reality differed slightly from the T01 inventory snapshot: `main.py` was already wrapping most of the legacy admin routers in `dependencies=[Depends(get_current_admin_user)]`, so the live app often looked safe even though the router modules themselves still declared only `get_current_user`. Rather than widen scope into a routing refactor, I tightened the module-level dependencies and added isolated-router proof so the boundary is explicit and reuse-safe. I also found that `release_verification_router` is imported in `main.py` but not currently mounted; I still hardened its module-level dependency and covered it with the same isolated-router proof instead of changing product exposure in this task.

## Known Issues

The repo-root pytest commands in this harness still emit the pre-existing pytest-cov warning `Failed to generate report: No data to report.` even when the tests pass; the security gate stays trustworthy because the command exits 0 and all targeted assertions are green. Also, `release_verification_router` remains unmounted in `backend/src/main.py`, so its new admin guard is currently proven through the isolated-router regression test rather than an app-mounted runtime path.

# T01: Added lifecycle race catalog and strict xfail proofs for stale pause/resume reopening terminal sessions

**Added lifecycle race catalog and strict xfail proofs for stale pause/resume reopening terminal sessions**

## What Happened

I read `SessionLifecycleService` plus the existing unit/integration lifecycle tests, then narrowed the concurrency focus to the two highest-risk regressions: (1) a stale `resume` reopening a sales session after `end` already converged it to `scoring`, and (2) a stale `pause` reopening a presentation session after `end` already converged it to `completed`. To make that target explicit for downstream work, I added `SESSION_LIFECYCLE_RACE_SCENARIOS` to `backend/src/common/db/session_lifecycle.py` as the shared proof catalog. I added a unit-level catalog assertion in `backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py` so future agents can see which transitions are intentionally prioritized. I then added a parameterized strict-`xfail` integration proof in `backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py` that binds two independent `AsyncSession` instances to the same engine, loads the same `PracticeSession` row twice, commits `end` in one session, and then commits `pause`/`resume` from the stale session. Running that proof with `--runxfail` showed the current regressions exactly as expected: `scoring -> in_progress` for sales and `completed -> paused` for presentation. After preserving the proof as strict `xfail`, the focused lifecycle suite stays green while still carrying the concrete red evidence T02 needs to turn passing. I also appended the stale-writer reproduction pattern to `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` so the next task can reuse the same harness directly.

## Verification

Verified the new proof in two modes. First, I ran the stale-writer proof with `--runxfail` to force the xfail test to fail normally and confirm the real race: the sales scenario reopened `scoring` back to `in_progress`, and the presentation scenario reopened `completed` back to `paused`. Second, I ran the focused lifecycle backend suite normally and confirmed it remains stable at `18 passed, 2 xfailed`, which preserves the failing proof without breaking the slice baseline. I also ran the task-plan `rg` verification and confirmed the new pause/resume/end/scoring/completed race catalog and proof references are present in the intended service and test files. LSP diagnostics for the touched Python files returned no issues.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -k 'lifecycle_race_proof_preserves_terminal_status_against_stale_writer' --runxfail -q` | 1 | ✅ pass (expected red proof exposed stale-writer regressions) | 2900ms |
| 2 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 4130ms |
| 3 | `rg -n "pause|resume|end|scoring|completed" backend/src/common/db/session_lifecycle.py backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py` | 0 | ✅ pass | 18ms |

## Deviations

Instead of leaving the repository in a permanently red state after proving the race, I converted the verified red proofs into `strict=True` xfail tests. This keeps T01 executable and preserves the evidence for T02 without blocking focused lifecycle verification.

## Known Issues

The focused pytest commands still emit existing repo-level warnings unrelated to this task: pytest-cov reports `Module src was never imported` / `No data was collected` on these focused runs, and the pre-existing `test_lifecycle_api_refreshes_live_session_activity_after_rest_transition` path emits a `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'Connection._cancel' was never awaited` while the suite still exits 0. No new product blocker was introduced.

## Files Created/Modified

- `backend/src/common/db/session_lifecycle.py`
- `backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py`
- `backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py`
- `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`

# T02: Added optimistic lifecycle convergence so stale pause/resume writes no longer reopen terminal sessions

**Added optimistic lifecycle convergence so stale pause/resume writes no longer reopen terminal sessions**

## What Happened

I promoted the T01 stale-writer proof from strict xfail into an enforced passing contract, then implemented the convergence logic inside `backend/src/common/db/session_lifecycle.py` instead of scattering guards across REST/WebSocket callers. The service now persists lifecycle mutations with an optimistic compare-and-swap on `PracticeSession.status` (`UPDATE ... WHERE session_id=:id AND status=:observed_status`). When that write loses a race, the service loads the latest persisted lifecycle row through an independent async session, logs `practice_session_lifecycle_concurrency_conflict`, syncs the in-memory session back to the durable state, and converges stale non-terminal writers that hit an already-terminal session (`scoring` / `completed`) into `changed=False` no-ops instead of reopening the session. For non-terminal conflicts, the service retries once from the persisted state so normal same-action/idempotent convergence still works. This keeps the existing terminal split intact (`sales -> scoring`, `presentation -> completed`) and leaves report/replay unlock semantics unchanged because only the stale overwrite window changed, not the terminal targets or downstream end-of-session behavior. I updated the unit tests to separate pure state-machine assertions from explicit conflict-path assertions, and I upgraded the integration race proof so it now requires the stale writer itself to observe terminal state and return a no-op before commit. I also recorded the strategy choice in decision D197 and appended the SQLite stale-snapshot gotcha to `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` for downstream slices.

## Verification

I first ran the promoted race proof before the fix and confirmed the expected red state: stale `resume` / `pause` transitions still returned `changed=True` and reopened terminal rows. After implementing the optimistic status-guarded write path, I reran the targeted unit conflict tests, the promoted integration race proof, and finally the full task-plan lifecycle suite. All three passed. I also ran LSP diagnostics on `backend/src/common/db/session_lifecycle.py`, `backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py`, and `backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py`; all returned `No diagnostics`. The focused pytest commands still emit existing repo-level warnings from pytest-cov (`Module src was never imported` / `No data was collected`), the existing `passlib` crypt deprecation warning, and the pre-existing `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'Connection._cancel' was never awaited` in `test_lifecycle_api_end_is_idempotent_and_logs_unified_terminal_context`, but none were introduced by this task and the suite exits 0.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py -k 'stale_terminal_writer_converges_to_persisted_terminal_noop or fresh_terminal_resume_still_raises_invalid_transition' -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 501ms |
| 2 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -k 'lifecycle_race_proof_preserves_terminal_status_against_stale_writer' -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 2565ms |
| 3 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -x -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3576ms |

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

Existing focused pytest warnings remain: pytest-cov reports `Module src was never imported` / `No data was collected` on these narrow commands, `passlib` emits a `crypt` deprecation warning, and `test_lifecycle_api_end_is_idempotent_and_logs_unified_terminal_context` still produces the pre-existing `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'Connection._cancel' was never awaited` while the suite passes. No new blocker was discovered.

# T03: Documented the lifecycle concurrency contract in focused tests and added a presentation terminal-split proof.

**Documented the lifecycle concurrency contract in focused tests and added a presentation terminal-split proof.**

## What Happened

I turned the stale-writer lifecycle behavior from implicit knowledge into explicit focused-test contract text in both lifecycle proof files. `backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py` now carries a `LIFECYCLE_CONCURRENCY_CONTRACT` block that states why this slice stays on optimistic compare-and-swap instead of row locks, which race scenarios are intentionally converged (`sales_end_beats_stale_resume`, `presentation_end_beats_stale_pause`), which terminal statuses are intentionally different by scenario (`sales -> scoring`, `presentation -> completed`), and what repo-root pytest command remains the long-term regression entrypoint. I added unit assertions that bind those statements back to the real race catalog and `SessionLifecycleService.terminal_status_for_scenario(...)` so the contract is executable instead of comment-only.

On the API side, `backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py` now carries a matching `LIFECYCLE_API_CONCURRENCY_CONTRACT` block plus a dedicated proof that presentation `end` settles directly to `completed` while sales keeps the `scoring` handoff semantics documented elsewhere. During TDD I first added contract assertions without the constants and verified the red `NameError` failure, then added the contract constants and the new proof until the targeted subset and full focused lifecycle suite both passed. I also appended the presentation timestamp gotcha to `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` so future agents do not mistake the existing aware/naive `PresentationCoachService.end_session()` issue for a lifecycle concurrency regression.

## Verification

I followed a red-green flow for the new contract checks: the first targeted run failed with `NameError` because the contract constants were not defined yet, which confirmed the new assertions were live. After adding the contract text and the presentation terminal-split proof, I reran the focused contract subset and it passed. I then reran the full task-plan verification command (`backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -x -q`) and it passed at `27 passed`. LSP diagnostics on both touched Python test files returned `No diagnostics`. The focused pytest commands still emit the pre-existing pytest-cov no-data warnings, the passlib `crypt` deprecation warning, and the existing `Connection._cancel` runtime warning in the idempotent-end integration test, but no new failures were introduced.

## Verification Evidence

|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -k 'contract_documents or terminal_split or presentation_completes_without_scoring_handoff' -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3105ms |
| 2 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py -x -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3846ms |
| 3 | `rg -n "LIFECYCLE_CONCURRENCY_CONTRACT|LIFECYCLE_API_CONCURRENCY_CONTRACT|presentation_completes_without_scoring_handoff" backend/tests/unit/test_session_lifecycle_service.py backend/tests/integration/test_session_lifecycle_api.py .gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` | 0 | ✅ pass | 35ms |

## Deviations

I initially tried to prove the presentation `end -> completed` contract through the real `PresentationCoachService.end_session()` path, but that surfaced a pre-existing aware/naive timestamp mismatch unrelated to lifecycle concurrency. To keep this task focused on lifecycle semantics rather than duration math in another module, I narrowed the new presentation API proof by stubbing the coach-service collaborator to a stable terminal result.

## Known Issues

The underlying `PresentationCoachService.end_session()` timezone-mismatch behavior in the SQLite-backed test environment remains unfixed and is now documented in `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`. Existing focused-suite warnings also remain: pytest-cov reports `Module src was never imported` / `No data was collected`, passlib emits a `crypt` deprecation warning, and `test_lifecycle_api_end_is_idempotent_and_logs_unified_terminal_context` still triggers the pre-existing `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'Connection._cancel' was never awaited` while the suite exits 0.

# T01: Locked the real practice websocket seam with boundary notes and focused reconnect/backpressure/interrupt proofs.

**Locked the real practice websocket seam with boundary notes and focused reconnect/backpressure/interrupt proofs.**

## What Happened

I inspected `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.ts` together with its focused hook tests and the surrounding `websocket/message-handlers` module to distinguish real orchestration responsibility from superficial file-size complexity. The live code already had one important split in place: inbound protocol application (`status`, `reconnected`, `interrupted`, `backpressure`) belongs in `websocket/message-handlers`, while the hook still legitimately owns transport lifecycle, reconnect retry budget, pending outbound flush, binary negotiation on connect, outbound audio pacing/backpressure buffering, and interrupt pre-cleanup across playback refs and browser speech synthesis. I codified that boundary directly in the hook header instead of doing a fake extract-by-size refactor. I then strengthened the focused tests so future agents can see the seam in behavior: the hook suite now proves binary negotiation happens at connect time, backpressure is an inbound resume signal plus hook-owned outbound buffering/flush, and interrupt pre-cleanup stays in the hook before backend confirmation. On the presentation-focused suite, I corrected the contract proof to match the real runtime truth line: `sendControl("start")` is only an outbound command, and `sessionStatus` / `aiState` advance only after inbound backend `status` confirms the session is actually in progress.

## Verification

Ran the exact task-plan inventory command to confirm reconnect/backpressure/interrupt/binary coverage now appears directly in the hook and focused tests. Ran the impacted websocket hook suites with Vitest; both `src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts` and `src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.presentation-flow.test.ts` passed (18/18). Re-ran LSP diagnostics on `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.ts`, `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts`, and `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.presentation-flow.test.ts`; all were clean.

# T02: Tightened practice websocket reconnect cleanup so stale queued interrupts do not replay after reconnect and interrupt clears local backpressure state.

**Tightened practice websocket reconnect cleanup so stale queued interrupts do not replay after reconnect and interrupt clears local backpressure state.**

## What Happened

I kept the work on the seam identified in T01 instead of doing another size-driven split. In `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.ts`, I tightened outbound orchestration around two stale-state hazards the focused tests exposed: queued outbound messages are now only replayed during the initial `connecting` handshake, and reconnect/failed phases are treated as a fresh transport epoch so stale control/interrupt intent does not leak onto the next socket. I also made `sendInterrupt(...)` own the full local backlog cleanup path by clearing queued outbound work plus local backpressure/slow-state flags when it discards buffered audio. On the proof side, I added two focused regression tests in `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts`: one locks that interrupt clears the learner-visible local backpressure state after dropping buffered audio, and the other locks that an interrupt fired against a dead socket is not replayed when reconnect opens a fresh connection. I then recorded the seam-level rule in `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` (D200), added the non-obvious reconnect-epoch gotcha to `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`, and updated the safe-grow continuity files so T03 can start from the tightened contract instead of the old T01 checkpoint.

## Verification

I followed a red-green cycle on the websocket hook before widening verification. First I ran `npm --prefix web test -- --run src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts` after adding the new contract tests; it failed exactly where expected, showing that interrupt left `isBackpressureActive`/`isNetworkSlow` true after dropping buffered audio and that an interrupt triggered during reconnect was replayed on the next socket. After tightening the hook, I reran the focused hook suite and it passed 17/17. Then I ran the exact task-plan verification command `npm --prefix web test -- --run "src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts" "src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.presentation-flow.test.ts" "src/app/(user)/practice/[sessionId]/page.test.tsx"`; all three suites passed (30/30), confirming the reconnect/backpressure/interrupt change preserved the presentation-flow and learner-page contracts. Fresh LSP diagnostics were clean on `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.ts`, `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.test.ts`, and `web/src/hooks/use-practice-websocket.presentation-flow.test.ts`.

tasks.narrative:
I traced the live presentation mutation authority in `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py` and wrote the discovery result back next to the runtime code as `PRESENTATION_RESOURCE_RACE_INVENTORY` plus `PRESENTATION_RESOURCE_RACE_FOCUS`. That inventory now separates three surfaces: new uploads are mostly isolated by fresh storage keys and atomic writes, in-place replace is the first concurrent-writer proof target because it mutates the stable `presentation_id` after only a read-then-write active-session preflight, and delete is an already-confirmed guard gap because the route only checks ownership/admin permission.

I locked that discovery seam with one focused contract assertion and one focused integration proof. The contract test now keeps the inventory categories and recommended next proof stable for later agents. The new integration test proves the current delete behavior directly: after creating a live presentation session, deleting the presentation still returns 204 and the session row remains persisted with its `presentation_id` detached to `None` in the shared test harness. I also recorded the pattern/priority choice in D201 and saved the non-obvious delete-path gotcha in `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` so downstream work can start T02 from the real race surface instead of re-auditing the same routes.

# T01: Added a code-adjacent presentation race inventory and focused proofs that lock concurrent in-place replace as the next target while proving delete currently lacks a route-level live-session blocker.

**Added a code-adjacent presentation race inventory and focused proofs that lock concurrent in-place replace as the next target while proving delete currently lacks a route-level live-session blocker.**

## What Happened

I traced the live presentation mutation authority in `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py` and wrote the discovery result back next to the runtime code as `PRESENTATION_RESOURCE_RACE_INVENTORY` plus `PRESENTATION_RESOURCE_RACE_FOCUS`. That inventory now separates three surfaces: new uploads are mostly isolated by fresh storage keys and atomic writes, in-place replace is the first concurrent-writer proof target because it mutates the stable `presentation_id` after only a read-then-write active-session preflight, and delete is an already-confirmed guard gap because the route only checks ownership/admin permission.

I locked that discovery seam with one focused contract assertion and one focused integration proof. The contract test now keeps the inventory categories and recommended next proof stable for later agents. The new integration test proves the current delete behavior directly: after creating a live presentation session, deleting the presentation still returns 204 and the session row remains persisted with its `presentation_id` detached to `None` in the shared test harness. I also recorded the pattern/priority choice in D201 and saved the non-obvious delete-path gotcha in `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` so downstream work can start T02 from the real race surface instead of re-auditing the same routes.

## Verification

Fresh backend verification passed on the touched presentation authority and proof files. `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py -q` finished 9/9 green, covering the existing replace contract/integration behavior plus the new discovery-focused inventory and delete guard-gap proof. The exact task-plan grep gate, `rg -n "replace|upload|delete|active-session|lock" backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py`, returned the expected inventory/proof lines. Fresh LSP diagnostics on `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py`, `backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py`, and `backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py` reported no issues.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3665ms |
| 2 | `rg -n "replace|upload|delete|active-session|lock" backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py` | 0 | ✅ pass | 23ms |

## Deviations

I slightly widened the planned inventory-only writeback into one focused delete-path proof in `backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py` because the live route code showed no session-state preflight on delete, and the slice goal required separating covered surfaces from already-observable guard gaps.

## Known Issues

Concurrent in-place replace without active sessions is still unproved and remains the highest-priority T02 target. The delete handler still has no route-level live-session blocker and still does not remove stored PPT/thumbnail artifacts; the new focused proof shows that, in the shared integration harness, delete can detach a live session from its presentation authority.

# T02: Added a focused concurrent-replace proof that confirms in-place presentation replace has a real writer race and updated the code-adjacent discovery inventory with the observed failure mode and next mitigation.

**Added a focused concurrent-replace proof that confirms in-place presentation replace has a real writer race and updated the code-adjacent discovery inventory with the observed failure mode and next mitigation.**

## What Happened

I started from the T01 inventory and wrote two new task-level proofs: a tighter contract assertion in `backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py` and a focused concurrent replace integration proof in `backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py`. The first red run exposed a misleading harness behavior: the shared `backend/tests/conftest.py::async_client` fixture injects one `AsyncSession` into both requests, so concurrent API calls there can fail for shared-session reasons before they say anything truthful about route-level races. I preserved that finding in `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`, then rebuilt the integration proof with a per-request `get_db` override backed by a shared file-based SQLite database so each replace request had its own real session.

With that corrected harness, the focused proof showed the actual replace race: two concurrent `/api/v1/presentations/{id}/replace` writers can both pass the active-session preflight and enter the version-2 path; the faster writer commits `version_number=2`, while the delayed writer falls into the global 500 fallback during page rebuild because `pages(presentation_id, page_number)` collides. I wrote that observed failure mode back into `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py` by upgrading `PRESENTATION_RESOURCE_RACE_INVENTORY` from `needs_focused_proof` to `confirmed_concurrent_writer_race`, and by adding the concrete recommendation to serialize in-place replace with a compare-and-swap or lock before any broader multi-writer rollout. The upload surface remains inventory-only, and the delete surface remains a separately confirmed route-guard gap rather than an imagined lock issue.

This task did not implement locking or retry logic. It only converted the highest-priority suspicion into a code-adjacent, test-backed discovery conclusion that future agents can cite directly.

## Verification

Fresh task-plan verification passed with `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_delete_permissions.py -x -q`, which finished 11/11 green. That run covers the new contract conclusion, the focused concurrent replace proof, the existing replace/delete presentation flow coverage, and the delete-permission boundary proof. I also ran fresh LSP diagnostics on `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py`, `backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py`, and `backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py`; all three reported no diagnostics.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `backend/venv/bin/python -m pytest -c backend/pyproject.toml backend/tests/contract/test_presentations.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_flow.py backend/tests/integration/test_presentation_delete_permissions.py -x -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 4220ms |

## Deviations

I slightly widened the planner’s test-file scope into a small writeback in `backend/src/presentation_coach/api/presentations.py` because T01 had already established the code-adjacent inventory as the canonical discovery artifact, and the focused proof result needed to be recorded there to actually distinguish confirmed races from audit guesses. I also replaced the default shared-session `async_client` harness with a per-request session override inside the focused integration proof after the first red run showed that the default fixture was only reproducing shared-session commit contention, not the route’s real concurrent conflict surface.

## Known Issues

Concurrent in-place replace is still unmitigated in production: the loser of the reproduced race still falls into the generic 500 fallback instead of a structured conflict response, and the focused proof emits an `SAWarning` because the stale writer tries to delete a page row the winner already removed. The delete route still has no live-session blocker and still does not remove stored PPT/thumbnail artifacts.

tasks.description:
Why: 先盘清当前已有文档、脚本和数据库连接方式，runbook 才不会写成脱离仓库现实的空文档。

Do:
1. 梳理当前部署方式、脚本、数据库连接方式与已知备份事实。
2. 找出 runbook 可以引用的真实命令、路径和证据位置。
3. 记录缺失项，但不在本任务里臆造自动化能力。

Done when: 已有一份 backup/recovery 现状清单，可直接支撑 runbook 编写。
tasks.verify: find docs scripts -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort | head -n 20
tasks.observability_impact: 形成 backup/recovery 当前现状与可引用路径清单。

tasks.title: 输出 backup/recovery baseline runbook
tasks.description:
Why: S03 的核心交付是把“缺失”变成一份最小可执行 runbook，而不是继续停留在 audit 描述。

Do:
1. 编写最小 runbook：备份频率、恢复步骤、验证步骤、负责人/证据位置、季度演练建议。
2. 只写当前真实现状，不写未落地的理想化运维平台。
3. 保留未来改进建议，但与当前可执行内容分开。

Done when: 仓库中出现一份可直接被人类执行的 backup/recovery baseline runbook。
tasks.observability_impact: backup/recovery 基线从 audit 描述变成可检查的仓库 artifact。

tasks.title: 校正 runbook 为真实可执行现状
tasks.description:
Why: runbook 如果不和真实命令、真实路径对齐，很快就会变成误导性文档。

Do:
1. 人工走查 runbook 引用的命令与路径。
2. 去掉空泛理想架构描述，只保留当前可执行现状。
3. 把未来演练建议单列为 follow-up，而不混进当前基线。

Done when: runbook 里的路径/命令都能在当前仓库语境下成立，grep proof 通过。
tasks.observability_impact: runbook 是否仍反映当前现实，可由文件存在和内容 grep 直接检查。

decisions.decision: 首发环境优先级
decisions.choice: 先把桌面端稳定性做满
decisions.rationale: 用户明确要求第一版先保证桌面端真实可用，不把移动端 / 企业微信首发绑进 M001
decisions.revisable: Yes — 当桌面端闭环已证明并计划扩展使用环境时

decisions.decision: 训练材料事实源
decisions.choice: 公司标准 PPT 与产品资料由后台知识库 / 资产管理维护，不写死在 prompt
decisions.rationale: 训练内容需要持续更新；业务侧必须能自己上传、更新、替换，并在下一次训练生效

decisions.decision: PPT 第一版教练方式
decisions.choice: 第一版先做完整讲完后的统一复盘，实时打断后置
decisions.rationale: 用户把会后统一总结定义为硬门槛，把实时纠偏定义为高价值增强项
decisions.revisable: Yes — 当实时纠偏在技术与体验上都被证明成立时

decisions.decision: 主管第一版使用方式
decisions.choice: 先提供单次报告 + 连续变化供主管线下辅导，不在系统内做任务派发
decisions.rationale: 用户明确接受第一版主管先看报告、线下带教；避免把组织化管理复杂度提前注入主链路
decisions.revisable: Yes — 当单次报告和趋势视图已可信后

decisions.decision: 首发集成边界
decisions.choice: 第一版先独立使用，不新增外部系统集成
decisions.rationale: 当前最重要的是证明训练闭环成立；过早接 SSO / CRM / 外部文档系统会放大验收复杂度
decisions.revisable: Yes — 当独立系统闭环已稳定且明确需要外部接入时

decisions.decision: 规划顺序原则
decisions.choice: 先证明训练闭环成立，再建设实时教练、知识真实性、复盘增强与规模化治理
decisions.rationale: 现有仓库已有大量骨架，真正短板在闭环可靠性与结果可信度，而不是页面数量

decisions.decision: 销售训练生命周期权威写入口
decisions.choice: 以 `SessionLifecycleService` + `POST /practice/sessions/{id}/lifecycle` 作为销售训练生命周期唯一权威写入口；保留旧 `DELETE /practice/sessions/{id}` 时必须委托同一结束实现
decisions.rationale: 当前结束、副作用与 live sync 在多个入口分叉，必须先收口才能稳定多轮与报告跳转
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若未来确有外部兼容需求，可保留多入口但仍需共享同一实现

decisions.decision: 销售重连恢复边界
decisions.choice: 销售 StepFun runtime 只恢复最小可继续会话状态（`session_status` / `ai_state` / `turn_count` / 最近活动）并复用 `SessionStateService` + `reconnected` 协议，不序列化上游 StepFun 连接内部状态
decisions.rationale: 降低恢复复杂度，同时让 sales 与 presentation 共用同一恢复协议和前端消费面
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续证明必须恢复更多 runtime 字段，再在同一协议上扩展

decisions.decision: 会话终态单写入口
decisions.choice: `POST /practice/sessions/{id}/lifecycle` 的 `end` 与旧 `DELETE /practice/sessions/{id}` 统一委托 `backend/src/common/api/practice.py` 内同一终态 helper，再由各入口只决定返回 lifecycle payload 还是 report payload
decisions.rationale: 终态副作用（sales summary、presentation score flush、report trigger、live handler sync、terminal close）必须共享实现，测试和日志才能收敛到一个排查面
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若未来新增兼容入口，也必须继续复用同一 helper

decisions.decision: Sales StepFun 重连快照边界
decisions.choice: Sales StepFun 只持久化 `session_status` / `ai_state` / `turn_count` 与继续训练所需的最小 runtime 字段（`current_request_id`、最近 stage、最新 scoring/action card），重连时主动清空活跃 response / tool call / grounding 缓冲等不可恢复上游状态
decisions.rationale: StepFun 上游连接和流式中间态不可安全序列化；把恢复边界收窄到“可继续训练”而不是“继续同一流”，才能保证断线重连与终态清理稳定可测
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续证明需要恢复更多字段，必须继续沿用最小可恢复 + 显式清理不可恢复态的模式

decisions.decision: 训练页前端生命周期权威面
decisions.choice: 训练页只把服务端 `status` / `reconnected` / `session_ended` 作为 `sessionStatus` / `aiState` / 报告跳转的权威来源；本地只做音频清理，结束失败必须留在训练页暴露重试/重连
decisions.rationale: 本地乐观改状态会把 pause/resume/end 漂移伪装成报告页问题；把跳转与状态面绑定到服务端终态，失败才能对用户和后续排障可见
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若未来扩展新的服务端 lifecycle 事件，客户端可以消费新合约，但仍不能回到本地猜测终态

decisions.decision: 训练会话事实基线
decisions.choice: 以 `PracticeSession` + `ConversationMessage` 的共享 session evidence projection 作为 report / replay / history / trends 的事实源，`ComprehensiveReport` 退为可选增强视图 / 缓存
decisions.rationale: 当前分数与证据在 quick report、replay、history、trends、comprehensive report 之间漂移；先统一读模型，才能证明“报告事实源一致”且不依赖 staged evaluation 是否存在
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续迁移到专用 evidence 表，可保留同一 projection contract 并让底层存储替换

decisions.decision: StepFun 销售证据写入规范
decisions.choice: 逐轮与终态写入一律规范化到 `score_snapshot.overall_score`；历史 `overall` 仅做兼容输入；零轮/薄证据会话写入显式 `evaluable=false` + `not_evaluable_reason=INSUFFICIENT_TURN_DATA`，不再把 summary 失败当作终态语义
decisions.rationale: 下游 report / replay / history / trends 只有在写入层先收稳键形与不可评估事实，才能共享同一事实基线并区分“证据不足”与“summary 真异常”
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若未来迁移到专用 evidence 表或扩展不可评估原因枚举，仍应保持同一显式终态语义

decisions.decision: 共享 session evidence projection 读合同
decisions.choice: 会话级 overall 一律按 `logic/accuracy/completeness` 三项平均输出；若顶层分数缺失，则从最新 message `score_snapshot` 回填三项并同步暴露 `evidence_completeness` / `legacy_score_key_used` 诊断；逐轮 snapshot 只对外暴露 canonical `overall_score` 读形
decisions.rationale: report / replay / history 必须共用同一 top-line 公式和 legacy fallback，不然即便同源读表仍会继续各算各的；同时把 completeness 与 legacy fallback 暴露出来，后续 agent 才能快速判断是旧数据还是 consumer 绕过 projection
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续引入专用 evidence 表或改变顶层 overall 公式，需要以同一 projection contract 版本化迁移，而不是让 consumer 各自重算

decisions.decision: 历史/统计/趋势聚合口径
decisions.choice: `HistoryService` 负责先把 completed session 投影成统一 history summary，再由 statistics/trends 只聚合 `evaluable=true` 的 completed sessions；history list 仍保留非 completed 行，但仅对 completed 行暴露 projection evidence 字段
decisions.rationale: 主管视图既要和 report/replay 用同一事实基线，又不能让 aborted / non-evaluable 会话把平均分和趋势线直接拉歪；把 completed/evaluable gating 固化在同一 service，后续 consumer 和前端才能稳定共享语义
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续前端需要展示 non-evaluable 趋势点，可在同一 projection contract 上扩展显式点类型，但不能回到另一套 score 公式

decisions.decision: Web 报告/回放/历史消费边界
decisions.choice: report / replay / history 页面只把统一 evidence contract 当作 top-line score、evaluable、not_evaluable_reason、stage_summary、evidence_completeness 的事实源；`ComprehensiveReport` 与 highlights 仅做可缺失的增强内容
decisions.rationale: 后端读模型已经统一后，前端若继续并行拉多接口再本地补分，用户仍会看到“假一致”的多套事实；把增强内容降级为可选层，才能让浏览器消费面和后端基线真正对齐
decisions.revisable: Yes — 若后续引入版本化前端 evidence contract，三页可以一起迁移，但仍必须继续遵守“一条事实线 + 可选增强层”

decisions.decision: How supervisor-facing admin surfaces should present and open single-session results
decisions.choice: Project completed admin sessions through SessionEvidenceService-backed preview fields and deep-link both admin user detail and manager-lite not_passed entries to the shared /practice/{sessionId}/report page
decisions.rationale: This keeps supervisors and learners on the same evidence baseline, removes legacy weighted-score drift from admin previews, and avoids creating a separate supervisor-only report surface that could diverge from the canonical session report.

decisions.decision: How supervisor-facing single-session previews and drill-in CTAs should be sourced
decisions.choice: Reuse SessionEvidenceService projection for completed admin session previews and deep-link all supervisor entry points to /practice/{sessionId}/report
decisions.rationale: This keeps admin users detail, manager-lite not_passed cards, and the learner report page on one authoritative evidence line instead of mixing legacy 0.4/0.3/0.3 summaries with a separate supervisor-specific view.

decisions.decision: How standard PPT replacement should work without mutating in-flight presentation sessions
decisions.choice: Replace standard PPTs in place on a stable `presentation_id`, increment `version_number`, rebuild page-scoped coaching data, and block replacement while non-terminal sessions reference that presentation
decisions.rationale: Current uploads create a new `presentation_id`, which breaks the promise that the next training automatically uses the updated standard PPT. Keeping a stable identity preserves existing user entrypoints, while blocking active-session replacement avoids mid-session context drift in a runtime that reads `Page` rows live.

decisions.decision: What authority should prove that updated sales knowledge materials took effect
decisions.choice: Use the persona-centered chain `persona_policy -> VoiceRuntimePolicyService.resolve_effective_policy(...) -> PracticeSession.voice_policy_snapshot -> /practice/sessions/{id}/knowledge-check` as the only sales-material effectiveness proof line
decisions.rationale: This is already the live runtime authority for knowledge bindings and retrieval diagnostics. Reusing it prevents agent-level KB drift and ensures 'next new session uses updated material' is proven by the same snapshot and report surfaces that the product already trusts.

decisions.decision: Which presentation API surface should the standard-PPT replace flow use
decisions.choice: Implement replace/version/blocker behavior on the live /api/v1/presentations surface and keep the existing admin pages consuming that contract
decisions.rationale: The shipped admin presentation detail page and user training selector already depend on api.presentations, while the legacy /admin/presentations backend is schema-stale and not the active runtime path. Extending the live surface kept the change small, preserved the stable presentation_id flow end-to-end, and avoided wiring new UI onto a dead contract.

decisions.decision: How knowledge material diagnostics should distinguish infrastructure failure from normal retrieval outcomes
decisions.choice: Return 503 for admin knowledge search backend outages and expose explicit knowledge-check statuses for search_failed and no_knowledge_base instead of collapsing them into miss/not_found semantics.
decisions.rationale: Admins need to self-diagnose whether a document update is merely not matching, not ready yet, or blocked by the retrieval/embedding infrastructure. Distinguishing these states keeps the admin knowledge page and session report diagnostics trustworthy and makes 'next new session uses updated material' debuggable without shell access.

decisions.decision: Which layer owns S05 销售价值表达 / 异议处理语义，以及哪些消费面需要同步调整
decisions.choice: 把 S05 实现为 write-layer sales rubric + persona instruction contract slice：在 StepFun realtime scoring/effectiveness 写入层和 persona policy 编译层落地新语义，只做最小 live/report consumer 对齐，保持 SessionEvidenceService 与 report/replay/history 继续作为只读事实消费面
decisions.rationale: S02 已经把 report/replay/history/trends 收口到统一 evidence projection，S04 已把材料绑定冻结到 voice_policy_snapshot/knowledge-check。若在 read-side 或 comprehensive-report enhancement 再发明第二套 sales scorer，会重新引入事实漂移。把语义改动压在 active StepFun write path，并只同步 ScorePanel/report 标签，既能让用户真的看到新训练目标，又不需要重做统一事实线。

decisions.decision: How S05 should change sales scoring semantics without breaking the unified evidence contract
decisions.choice: Preserve existing top-level report/evidence field names and legacy pass_flag subkeys, but remap their meaning to sales value-articulation, evidence-benefit, and objection-progression semantics via shared write-layer helpers
decisions.rationale: Report/replay/history/admin consumers already depend on SessionReport.logic_score/accuracy_score/completeness_score and pass_flags.* keys. Renaming keys in S05 would create unnecessary read-side churn and cross-slice regressions. Keeping the contract stable while moving the semantics in realtime scoring, StepFun flush, and terminal fallback lets the product switch to sales-specific facts without reintroducing projection drift.

decisions.decision: How sales-focused persona prompts should steer knowledge retrieval without creating a second materials path
decisions.choice: Normalize sales-focus persona extensions centrally in persona_policy, compile them into the existing voice instruction contract, and widen price/competitor/proof retrieval through stepfun_knowledge_helpers query tuning.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the S04 authority line intact (`persona_policy -> resolve_effective_policy -> voice_policy_snapshot`) while making sales objection prompts materially stronger. Reusing the existing helper avoids a parallel retrieval contract and keeps knowledge-check/runtime diagnostics on one inspection surface.

decisions.decision: How S06 continuous-change aggregation and admin score summaries should be sourced
decisions.choice: Implement S06 as a projection-backed supervisor snapshot in `HistoryService`, and have `GET /api/v1/admin/users/{id}/progress` plus the score-bearing fields in `GET /api/v1/admin/users/{id}/stats` read that snapshot instead of route-level 0.4/0.3/0.3 SQL averages.
decisions.rationale: S02/S03 already proved that report, replay, history, and completed-session previews must share one evidence line. Leaving `/progress` or `/stats` on raw weighted SQL would keep mixed truths on the same admin page. Reusing `HistoryService` and the existing `SessionEvidenceService` projection also lets S06 aggregate repeated `main_issue.issue_type` / `next_goal.goal_type` buckets and explicit not-evaluable counts without inventing a second supervisor-only scorer.

decisions.decision: How admin user detail score-bearing stats and supervisor progress should aggregate completed sessions
decisions.choice: Route both `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/stats` score fields and `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/progress` through new HistoryService projection-backed helpers instead of route-local weighted SQL
decisions.rationale: Admin user detail already mixes completed-session previews built from SessionEvidenceService with legacy route-local score math, which produced drifting truths on the same page. Centralizing the score summary and supervisor snapshot in HistoryService keeps stats/progress aligned with the shared evidence projection, exposes not-evaluable semantics explicitly, and lets granularity/repeated-issue logging live in one place.

decisions.decision: How S07 should deliver PPT post-session review without breaking the unified evidence baseline
decisions.choice: Keep `/api/v1/practice/sessions/{id}/report` as the canonical single-session report entrypoint, add `scenario_type` plus a presentation-specific `presentation_review` payload sourced from `PresentationReportService`, and make the shared web report page branch on `scenario_type` instead of reusing sales-only pass flags or knowledge-check surfaces.
decisions.rationale: S02 already established one authoritative evidence line for report/replay/history/trends. Letting PPT core facts live only in `/evaluation/.../report` or remapping them into sales-shaped keys would recreate truth drift. Reusing `PresentationReportService` preserves the existing deterministic PPT scorer, while a scenario-aware shared contract lets the user-facing `/practice/{id}/report` remain the only canonical entrypoint for both sales and presentation sessions.

decisions.decision: What component owns normalized PPT post-session review facts and how legacy runtime page evidence is persisted
decisions.choice: Use PresentationReportService as the single builder for normalized presentation review payloads, and pass legacy current_page through transcript_metadata.page_number on the existing MessageStorageService.update_analysis path
decisions.rationale: Shared report and enhanced report need one presentation-review authority to avoid diverging heuristics, while reusing the existing message-analysis update path keeps legacy and StepFun runtimes on the same persisted page-evidence fact line with explicit degraded diagnostics when historical page metadata is missing.

decisions.decision: Which product surface and truth source should own M001/S08 release health and final launch proof
decisions.choice: Extend `/support/runtime` with an evidence-backed release-health read model sourced from persisted session evidence, shared runtime diagnostics, and canonical report semantics, then use that surface plus `S08-UAT.md` as the M001 launch gate instead of relying on raw `SystemLog` counts or the generic `release_verification` subsystem.
decisions.rationale: The current support/runtime surface can report false-green health because it counts `status="scoring"` as completed and misses most milestone-critical anomalies. Reusing `SessionEvidenceService`, knowledge-check semantics, and presentation degraded reasons keeps S08 on the same evidence line as report, trends, and PPT review while giving support/admin a small, truthful, in-product release surface. This is the smallest safe change that closes observability and launch proof without creating a second verification console.

decisions.decision: How support/runtime should classify release health once the evidence-backed reader replaces the old SystemLog overview
decisions.choice: Use persisted session evidence plus shared runtime diagnostics as the primary release-health truth line; treat `stuck_scoring`, `projection_failed`, `not_evaluable_completed`, `knowledge_search_failed`, and `kb_lock_blocked_*` as blocking anomalies, while `presentation_degraded_missing_page_metadata`, `optional_report_failed`, `upstream_unstable`, and raw SystemLog rows remain warning-level surfaces.
decisions.rationale: The release surface must stop reporting false-green health when sessions are stuck in scoring or canonical evidence/report semantics are broken. Blocking severity is reserved for anomalies that invalidate the core training/report truth line, while degraded-but-readable PPT reports, optional enhanced-report failures, transient upstream instability, and legacy logs stay visible without outweighing the canonical evidence signal.

decisions.decision: S01 realtime sales-contract scope across runtime modes
decisions.choice: Treat both StepFun and user-selectable classic voice mode as supported S01 runtime surfaces, align both to the same five-dimension practice-page sales contract, and keep the report contract as the existing three sales rollups.
decisions.rationale: The agent launch page still exposes classic mode, so a StepFun-only fix would leave a visible drift path on the training page. Aligning classic mode through the shared sales effectiveness helper closes that gap without reopening the M001 report/evidence baseline, which intentionally compresses realtime dimensions into three read-side rollups.

decisions.decision: Which helper is authoritative for classic sales realtime action-card semantics
decisions.choice: Use build_sales_effectiveness_metrics(...) + evaluate_pass_flags(...) in CapabilityProcessor instead of reconstructing pass flags from communication/structure heuristics
decisions.rationale: Classic voice mode is still a user-reachable runtime surface. Reusing the shared sales effectiveness helper keeps its action-card semantics aligned with StepFun and the existing three-rollup report contract without changing websocket field names or read-side consumers.

decisions.decision: What defines idempotence for practice-page sales score_update payloads
decisions.choice: Treat the full ScoreUpdate payload (overall_score, turn_count, stage_name, suggestions, dimension_scores, session_id) as the frontend idempotence boundary instead of deduping only on overall_score + turn_count.
decisions.rationale: Sales realtime feedback can refine stage guidance and dimension-level coaching within the same turn. If the practice page drops those same-turn refreshes, the ScorePanel silently shows stale sales advice even though the backend contract already advanced.

decisions.decision: Where S02 should enforce prompt pacing, single-turn action priority, and stale-hint clearing
decisions.choice: Use one shared realtime feedback arbitration helper after capability execution for both classic and StepFun runtime paths, keep capability-local cooldowns intact, persist only the minimum pacing state needed across reconnects, and make the frontend treat `action_card` as the primary coaching surface while clearing turn-bound hints on new final transcripts.
decisions.rationale: The current problem is cross-channel competition after fuzzy/stage/score signals are generated, not the low-level capability math itself. A shared post-capability arbiter keeps S01's canonical sales payload stable across both runtime modes, avoids duplicating priority logic in two handlers, and gives S02 a single seam for cooldowns, dedupe, and reconnect-safe pacing. Frontend precedence and transcript-driven clearing then prevent stale or duplicate coaching from lingering into later turns without inventing a second heuristic policy on the client.

decisions.decision: How realtime coaching signals are arbitrated across backend runtimes
decisions.choice: Introduce a shared realtime-feedback arbiter that preserves fuzzy/stage/score payloads as context, prefers scoring guidance over low-severity fuzzy detections for the primary action card, and suppresses duplicate action cards only when the same signature repeats within the same turn.
decisions.rationale: S02 needs one backend seam before classic and StepFun can converge on the same pacing rules. The arbiter removes competing coaching from the primary surface without breaking S01 payload fields or FuzzyDetectionCapability's local cooldown semantics, and its minimal signature/turn state is small enough to persist for reconnect-safe StepFun reuse in T02.

decisions.decision: What reconnect-safe StepFun metadata should be persisted alongside read-side realtime diagnostics
decisions.choice: Persist a nested `feedback_pacing_state` derived from `RealtimeFeedbackPacingState` while keeping `_latest_score_snapshot` and `_latest_action_card` as read-side diagnostics
decisions.rationale: The arbiter only needs last action signature plus turn number to suppress same-turn replay bursts after restore. Persisting that minimal state keeps StepFun aligned with classic pacing without bloating the reconnect snapshot or replacing the existing score/action diagnostics that later slices still inspect.

decisions.decision: How S03 should unify next-turn coaching across stage, score, and action-card surfaces
decisions.choice: Introduce one shared backend coaching-focus resolver in `common.effectiveness`, keep the websocket `action_card` payload stable, feed rich stage/score context from both classic and StepFun runtimes into the arbiter, and defer replay/report schema changes to later slices.
decisions.rationale: The current drift comes from `build_action_card(...)` choosing next-turn rules from pass flags alone while StepFun strips the richer stage and score context already available on the classic path. Centralizing the rule in backend shared logic keeps the practice page as a renderer, reuses S02's single-arbiter architecture, avoids reopening S01/S02 payload contracts, and gives S04 a stable seam for later report alignment without forcing an early persistence migration.

decisions.decision: How T01 should introduce the shared stage-aware coaching-focus rule without breaking current runtime callers
decisions.choice: Expose resolve_sales_coaching_focus in common.effectiveness and have build_action_card switch to that helper only when rich stage/score context is supplied; keep the legacy fuzzy/suggestion + pass-flags fallback for unwired callers until T02/T03 pass full context.
decisions.rationale: This isolates S03/T01 to the shared backend rule seam, keeps the websocket action_card shape unchanged, lets the new focused coaching contract land with tests immediately, and avoids forcing classic/StepFun runtime wiring changes before the later tasks that explicitly own them.

decisions.decision: How S04 should align realtime coaching with report/replay conclusions
decisions.choice: Use a shared read-side alignment helper in `common.effectiveness`, apply it as a projection-only override in `SessionEvidenceService` for completed sales sessions, keep the public report/replay/websocket contracts stable, and make replay render the existing `main_issue` / `next_goal` directly instead of introducing new persistence or client-side heuristics.
decisions.rationale: S03 already established one authoritative realtime coaching seam in `resolve_sales_coaching_focus(...)`, while the remaining drift lives in read-side consumers that reuse stale `effectiveness_snapshot` values. A projection-only override closes report/replay/history/admin drift with the smallest blast radius, avoids a DB migration, preserves the S01/S03 websocket contract, and lets replay visibly confirm the same conclusion family the report shows.

decisions.decision: How the shared sales report-alignment seam should expose diagnosable state without changing the public report contract
decisions.choice: Add `resolve_sales_report_alignment(...)` in `common.effectiveness` that returns existing `main_issue` / `next_goal` payloads plus internal `alignment_used`, `stage_key`, `focus_type`, and `fallback_reason` diagnostics, and reuse the same report vocabulary map for both direct sales evaluation and the new alignment helper.
decisions.rationale: S04 needs one read-side seam that later projection/logging tasks can inspect for override/fallback behavior without adding new API keys, websocket fields, or persistence columns. Returning internal diagnostics now keeps downstream observability simple while ensuring the aligned helper and the existing sales evaluator cannot drift onto different issue/goal vocabularies.

decisions.decision: How SessionEvidenceService should choose persisted sales evidence when newer messages only carry partial snapshots
decisions.choice: Projection-side sales alignment should scan backward for the latest message whose persisted stage+score pair can actually align (`alignment_used=True`) and use that message for `main_issue` / `next_goal` overrides; if no such message exists, keep the existing snapshot and log the fallback reason.
decisions.rationale: Completed sales sessions can contain newer messages that only persist `overall_score` while the last full-dimension sales evidence lives on an earlier turn. Using the newest raw snapshot would silently disable alignment and reintroduce report/replay drift even though persisted aligned evidence still exists. Selecting the latest alignable message preserves the shared sales-first conclusion baseline while keeping insufficient-evidence fallbacks explicit and unchanged.

decisions.decision: What planning boundary should govern future roadmap and slice work after the 2026-03-25 hard steer
decisions.choice: Anchor milestone and slice planning to real business-code entrypoints, core modules, and runnable product directories; do not elevate environment/tooling artifacts into milestones unless the explicit goal is environment migration; if the real code entry chain cannot be located, stop and do inventory/spike before execution
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly overrode the prior roadmap direction because it was drifting away from the actual user value chain. Future plans must start from existing admin/user entrypoints and authority modules, keep acceptance tied to those runtime surfaces, and avoid burning milestone scope on Conda/.env/lockfile-style work that does not directly move the product loop.

decisions.when_context: M005 S01 planning after hard steer
decisions.decision: What sequencing should govern M005 planning on the current admin business chain
decisions.choice: Start M005 by replacing legacy weighted admin analytics semantics with the current projection/evidence line on existing admin routes before adding richer manager workflow or governance features
decisions.rationale: `backend/src/common/analytics/admin_analytics_service.py` still shows legacy weighted-score logic, while admin user detail and learner/report surfaces already rely on projection-backed semantics. If new manager workflow or governance work lands before that truth line is corrected, the organization layer will amplify bad signals instead of helping users.

decisions.when_context: M005 S01 planning after hard steer
decisions.decision: What sequencing should govern M005 planning on the current admin business chain
decisions.choice: Start M005 by replacing legacy weighted admin analytics semantics with the current projection/evidence line on existing admin routes before adding richer manager workflow or governance features
decisions.rationale: `backend/src/common/analytics/admin_analytics_service.py` still shows legacy weighted-score logic, while admin user detail and learner/report surfaces already rely on projection-backed semantics. If new manager workflow or governance work lands before that truth line is corrected, the organization layer will amplify bad signals instead of helping users.

decisions.when_context: M004 S01 planning after hard steer (appended after decision-id collision)
decisions.decision: Which product surfaces should own M004 learning-loop work after the 2026-03-25 hard steer
decisions.choice: Build M004 learning-loop enhancements on the existing report, replay, history, and highlight entrypoints instead of creating a new learning center or a second evaluator
decisions.rationale: The hard steer requires future planning to stay on real business-code entrypoints and user value chains. The repo already has authoritative report/replay/highlight routes and a unified session-evidence line; deepening those existing surfaces compounds user value without splitting facts or adding a parallel learning product.

decisions.when_context: M003 S01 planning after hard steer
decisions.decision: What sequencing should govern M003 planning on the current realism chain
decisions.choice: Start M003 by inventorying the real admin Persona/knowledge entry chain and locking one objection-query evidence vocabulary on current runtime/report surfaces before any richer Persona model or retrieval work proceeds
decisions.rationale: The hard steer requires planning to begin from real business entrypoints and to stop if the code path cannot be located. In this repo, the highest-risk failure mode is assuming admin config already affects runtime in a provable way. Locking the actual config → runtime → report chain first prevents later M003 slices from drifting into prompt theory or tooling noise.

decisions.decision: How the 2026-03-25 override resolves remaining M003 planning drift after the earlier hard steer
decisions.choice: Treat M003 as the current admin Persona detail / admin knowledge detail → practice session creation → voice_policy_snapshot/runtime retrieval → knowledge-check/report/replay business chain. This supersedes any residual M003 wording from earlier planning that still used invented live status names, abstract prompt-first scope, or environment/tooling work outside that runnable chain.
decisions.rationale: The earlier hard steer set the right direction, but the remaining M003 documents still mixed real routes/modules with non-live vocabulary such as `no_bound_kb` / `weak_evidence` and left enough ambiguity for roadmap scope to drift again. Narrowing the milestone to the confirmed business chain keeps acceptance tied to real code, protects user-value focus, and prevents future slices from burning scope on Conda/.env/lockfile-style work that does not move the product loop.

decisions.decision: Which backend routes own the accepted M003 proof surfaces for report/knowledge-check versus replay
decisions.choice: Keep session creation, knowledge-check, and report proof on `backend/src/common/api/practice.py`, but treat replay proof as the current `GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/replay` route in `backend/src/common/conversation/api.py` backed by `backend/src/common/conversation/replay.py` over `SessionEvidenceService`.
decisions.rationale: The current learner report page already depends on the unified `/practice/sessions/{id}/report` plus knowledge-check routes, while the learner replay page is served through the conversation replay API and only shares the read-side fact line via `SessionEvidenceService`. Flattening replay into `practice.py` would point later tests and live UAT at the wrong backend seam and make placeholder ownership look real.

decisions.decision: How Persona pressure behavior should be represented during the M003/S02 migration
decisions.choice: Store a canonical nested `customer_pressure` model inside `persona_policy`, keep `sales_focus` / `value_axes` / `objection_axes` / `expected_customer_questions` as derived compatibility projections, and flag raw rows that still lack the nested model as `pressure_model_legacy_only` in policy-health audits.
decisions.rationale: T02 and T03 need one structured contract they can freeze into session snapshots and expose on admin surfaces, but the current runtime/compiler/tests still read the flat sales-focus fields. Deriving the flat fields from the nested model preserves the existing authority chain while giving audits a clear way to distinguish truly legacy rows from rows that have already been normalized and persisted.

decisions.decision: How the M003/S02 runtime snapshot should expose Persona pressure behavior
decisions.choice: Expose `customer_pressure` as a top-level effective-policy/session-snapshot field, keep `persona_policy.customer_pressure` as the canonical nested source, and tag `source.customer_pressure_source` for inspection
decisions.rationale: T02 needs one runtime-facing pressure contract that the compiler, session creation path, and later inspection surfaces can read without reinterpreting prose instructions or live admin config. Mirroring the nested model onto the effective policy keeps the existing persona authority chain intact while making frozen session snapshots directly auditable.

decisions.decision: How current admin Persona pages should expose and persist the pressure model
decisions.choice: Reuse the existing `/admin/personas/policy-health` audit on the list page, and edit/persist `persona_policy.customer_pressure` on the detail page while also mirroring the flat legacy sales-focus fields for compatibility.
decisions.rationale: The task explicitly forbids a new management surface. Using the existing policy-health endpoint keeps audit on the current list page, while the detail page remains the single editing surface. Writing both the nested pressure model and the compatibility mirrors avoids downstream drift for consumers that still read the legacy flat keys.

decisions.decision: How to persist the cross-turn unresolved-objection ledger without adding a new store
decisions.choice: Reuse existing message transcript metadata and runtime snapshot state: persist the structured ledger under ConversationMessage.transcript_metadata["objection_ledger"] and mirror the same normalized dict in StepFun session runtime_state["objection_ledger"].
decisions.rationale: This keeps the ledger on the current runtime/evidence chain, avoids schema churn, and uses a read-side field that already passes through replay/report projections without changing the public score contract. Reconnect recovery also stays minimal because the same normalized dict can ride inside the existing session snapshot.

decisions.decision: What reconnect state should preserve unresolved-objection pressure for sales realtime sessions
decisions.choice: Persist the normalized objection ledger plus feedback pacing state, and stop depending on replaying the last action card from reconnect snapshots
decisions.rationale: The ledger carries the unresolved business gap across topic drift and reconnect, while feedback pacing state suppresses same-turn duplicate coaching. Persisting only these reconnect-safe facts avoids replaying stale action-card UI state while keeping the runtime pressure contract intact on the next turn.

decisions.decision: How read-side sales issue/goal surfaces should resolve when an unresolved objection ledger is still open
decisions.choice: Prefer the latest open transcript-metadata objection ledger over generic score-stage alignment when projecting main_issue and next_goal
decisions.rationale: The websocket runtime already persists one normalized unresolved-objection ledger specifically to survive topic drift and reconnect. If report/replay/history fall back to the last score-stage alignment, completed sessions can drift back to next-step or generic gaps and stop explaining the proof request that actually blocked the conversation. Reusing the existing main_issue/next_goal fields with ledger-backed text keeps the contract stable while carrying the real blocker forward.

decisions.decision: How realtime feedback should stay anchored to one unresolved objection when score focus drifts later in the same session
decisions.choice: Inject objection-ledger-derived synthetic objection stage/score context into the shared feedback arbiter, while keeping the persisted/public `score_update` snapshot on the real scoring contract
decisions.rationale: The product needs the AI customer to keep pressing the same unresolved proof gap across topic drift and reconnect, but changing the public score payload would reintroduce contract drift across practice, report, replay, and admin. Using ledger-derived override context keeps pressure stable at the arbiter seam without inventing a second runtime store or mutating the outward score contract.

decisions.decision: How to carry S04 claim-truth semantics through the current sales evaluator and read-side projection
decisions.choice: Store the canonical truth state as `effectiveness_snapshot.claim_truth` and let `SessionEvidenceService` overlay it from score-alignment and objection-ledger facts without renaming `main_issue` or `next_goal`.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the truth contract on the existing evaluator/session-evidence authority line, preserves stable report/replay keys for current consumers, and lets later runtime/report/replay work read one explicit status (`unsupported_claim`, `weak_evidence`, `evidence_pending`, `evidence_verified`) from the same snapshot even when issue/goal wording is overridden by newer evidence.

decisions.decision: How S04 runtime claim-truth should surface without creating a new debug contract or mutating the stable persisted score_snapshot shape
decisions.choice: Emit claim_truth on the existing StepFun score_update websocket payload, cache the latest live claim_truth on the handler/reconnect snapshot, and have /practice/sessions/{id}/knowledge-check read live handler state first with completed-session projection fallback.
decisions.rationale: The product already has two accepted runtime inspection seams: score_update on the live practice page and knowledge-check on the report/runtime diagnostics path. Reusing them exposes the new truth contract immediately, keeps _latest_score_snapshot and persisted score_snapshot stable for existing consumers, and avoids trying to persist extra score fields through StepFun message helpers that intentionally normalize the public snapshot shape.

decisions.decision: How learner-facing report and replay should source and display S04 claim-truth states
decisions.choice: Read claim-truth from the unified completed-session `effectiveness_snapshot.claim_truth` on report/replay and format it through a shared frontend helper, instead of borrowing `/knowledge-check` runtime diagnostics as the primary truth line.
decisions.rationale: The completed-session report and replay routes already share SessionEvidenceService-backed evidence contracts. Sourcing the learner-facing truth badge from `effectiveness_snapshot.claim_truth` keeps those read surfaces on the same canonical fact line, while letting runtime-only diagnostics like kb-lock chain failure remain separate in `/knowledge-check` instead of leaking infrastructure state into completed-session coaching copy.

decisions.decision: What counts as shippable degradation versus release-blocking failure for the M003 same-session objection-heavy proof
decisions.choice: Projection-backed canonical report degradation is shippable when it stays truthful and explicit, but replay/highlights blocked by a session stuck in scoring is release-blocking.
decisions.rationale: The S05 live same-session proof showed the report route still builds a truthful evidence projection and the report page keeps the learner on an inspectable evidence surface even when enhanced insights/highlights fail. The same proof also showed replay/highlights remain blocked with SESSION_NOT_COMPLETED after report_generation_failed [NO_STAGE_RESULTS], and replay is one of the accepted M003 proof surfaces. Explicit replay failure is useful observability, but it does not satisfy final milestone acceptance.

decisions.decision: How explanation-rich replay/highlight evidence is carried on the backend authority line
decisions.choice: Expose replay/highlight learning context through a nested `learning_evidence` object derived from `SessionEvidenceService` projection, while preserving flat compatibility fields (`sales_stage`, `stage_name`, `context`, `suggested_response`).
decisions.rationale: The nested object gives later slices one stable contract for deep links and retry intent without introducing a second scorer or freeform learning generator, and keeping the flat fields avoids breaking the existing replay/highlight UI while that adoption happens incrementally.

decisions.decision: How the replay/highlight UI should consume the new learning-evidence contract
decisions.choice: Bind replay page and highlight components to the shared web API `HighlightItem`/`ReplayLearningEvidence` types instead of maintaining separate local highlight interfaces.
decisions.rationale: T01 established a nested backend learning-evidence payload. Reusing the shared API types on the replay/highlight surfaces keeps the frontend on the same authority line, preserves fallback fields like `stage_name` and `suggested_response`, and reduces drift risk for the follow-up report/history work in T03.

decisions.decision: How report and history surfaces should derive user-facing learning cues for completed sessions
decisions.choice: Derive issue/goal coaching cues from the shared session-evidence contract (`main_issue` / `next_goal`) through a shared frontend helper, while treating highlights and enhanced reports as optional overlays.
decisions.rationale: Replay/highlight already use the unified evidence line for explanation-rich coaching. Reusing the same issue/goal vocabulary on report and history prevents conflicting copy when highlights are absent or enhanced report generation degrades, and keeps the current entrypoints aligned without adding a second truth line.

decisions.decision: Where replay deep-link anchors should live for report issue/goal navigation
decisions.choice: Attach a nested `replay_anchor` payload to `replay.main_issue` and `replay.next_goal`, derived from existing replay messages and timeline markers, with `status`/`degraded_reason` for fallback visibility.
decisions.rationale: This keeps anchor resolution on the same backend authority line that already owns replay conclusions, avoids introducing a parallel deep-link resolver or second evidence model, and lets the replay page surface degraded states such as missing highlights or markers without silently dropping the target.

decisions.decision: How report-page replay deep links should target the existing replay route
decisions.choice: Encode report deep links as replay-route query params (`focus`, `message_id`, `turn`, `anchor_status`, `anchor_reason`, `marker_type`, `marker_timestamp_ms`) and let highlight cards fall back to `focus+turn` when only a turn target is available.
decisions.rationale: T02 needs a stable handoff from the current report page into the existing replay route without adding a second evidence model. Reusing the replay anchor payload from the replay API keeps issue/goal navigation aligned to the canonical SessionEvidenceService projection, while a lightweight turn-only fallback keeps highlight-card navigation working on the current HighlightList seam until T03 consumes the query contract.

decisions.decision: How the current replay page should consume report deep-link anchors
decisions.choice: Parse the existing replay query params on the replay page, scroll to the requested turn when it still exists, and render a persistent resolved/degraded/missing anchor banner based on the query plus timeline marker lookup.
decisions.rationale: This keeps report→replay navigation on the existing route, makes degraded paths visible instead of silently failing when highlights or markers drift, and gives focused replay tests a stable UI contract for resolved and missing-target cases.

decisions.decision: How the M004/S03 retry focus should travel from completed-session evidence into a new practice session
decisions.choice: Expose a sales-only `retry_entry.focus_intent` on report responses and persist accepted create-session focus under `voice_policy_snapshot.focus_intent` on the new session
decisions.rationale: This keeps the authority line on the existing report and practice APIs, avoids inventing a second retry-launch store, and gives the later practice-page/runtime descriptor work a stable carry-forward seam on the already-frozen session snapshot.

decisions.decision: How the learner practice page should read carry-forward retry focus
decisions.choice: Expose carry-forward retry focus on `runtime_descriptor.focus_intent` and have the practice page consume that typed runtime descriptor field instead of reading raw `voice_policy_snapshot` directly.
decisions.rationale: The existing `/practice/sessions/{id}` session fetch already returns a canonical runtime descriptor. Extending that descriptor keeps the learner practice entry chain on one stable metadata surface, reuses the sanitized focus intent persisted at create-session time, and avoids adding a second fetch or frontend-only snapshot parsing path.

decisions.decision: How replay should source and execute the focused retry CTA in M004/S03
decisions.choice: Load retry metadata from canonical report `retry_entry` and reuse the existing `api.practice.createSession` + `/practice/{sessionId}` entry chain on both report and replay pages
decisions.rationale: This keeps report and replay aligned to one retry-launch contract, avoids inventing a replay-specific frontend flow, and lets `focus_intent` travel in the create-session body while scenario selection remains visible on the existing practice-route query params.

decisions.decision: How PPT page-level learning issues should travel on the existing report authority line
decisions.choice: Attach per-page issue clusters under presentation_review.page_summaries[*].issue_clusters, and expose only aggregate cluster count/types through diagnostics and projection completeness.
